questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word "Supersede."
questions.explanation: A) Supersede (verb) – replace, displace, supplant, override, discard. (स्थान लेना) Synonym: Supplant (verb) – Supersede and replace. (हटाकर स्थान लेना) • Supplicate (verb): To ask or beg for something earnestly or humbly. (प्रार्थना करना) • Suppurate (verb): To undergo the formation of pus; fester. (पकना) • Suppress (verb): To forcibly put an end to something. (दबाना)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word “Expedite.”
questions.explanation: A) Expedite (verb) - accelerate, hasten, precipitate, quicken, facilitate शीघ्रता करना Synonym: Precipitate (verb) – To cause an event or situation, typically one that is undesirable, to happen suddenly, unexpectedly, or prematurely. (तेजी लाना / जल्दबाज़ी करना) • Pulverize (verb): To reduce to fine particles; to utterly defeat or smash. (चूर-चूर करना) • Pontificate (verb): To express one's opinions in a way considered annoyingly pompous. (बड़बोलापन दिखाना) • Plagiarize (verb): To take the work or an idea of someone else and pass it off as one's own. (साहित्यिक चोरी)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate MEANING of the phrasal verb “Brush under the carpet.”
options: To hide or ignore a problem in the hope that it will be forgotten
To hire or ignore a project in the hope that it will be forgotten
To hint or ignore a process in the hope that it will be forgotten
To hide or ignore a profile in the hope that it will be forgotten
questions.explanation: A) Brush under the carpet (idiom) – To hide or ignore a problem in the hope that it will be forgotten. (मामले को दबा देना)

questions.question: From the alternatives given below, select the correct meaning of the idiom. Throw one a curveball.
options: To be behind or in advance of the current trend
To aggressively force one's way through some task or thing, especially clumsily or carelessly
To play baseball with, or throw a baseball back and forth with someone
To do something unexpected or deceptive that surprises, confuses, thwarts, or outwits someone
questions.explanation: D) Throw one a curveball (idiom) – To do something unexpected or deceptive that surprises or confuses someone अचानक चौंका देना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word "Dire."
questions.explanation: C) Dire (adjective) – terrible, dreadful, appalling, calamitous, grim. (भीषण/अत्यंत गंभीर) Antonym: Auspicious (adjective) – Conducive to success; favorable. (शुभ/अनुकूल) • Auriculars (noun): Feathers covering the ear of a bird. (कान के पास के पंख) • Aureating (verb/adj): Relating to the use of a gold color or ornate style. (स्वर्णमयी बनाना / अलंकृत करना) • Auspices (noun): A divine or prophetic token; support or patronage. (संरक्षण / तत्वावधान)

questions.question: . Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word “Isolated.”
questions.explanation: B) Isolated (adjective) – Solitary, remote, separate. (अलग-थलग) Antonym: Integrated (adjective) – With various parts or aspects linked or coordinated. (एकीकृत) • Inebriated (adjective): Drunk; intoxicated. (नशे में) • Incinerated (verb): Destroyed by burning. (जलाकर राख कर देना) • Infiltrated (verb): Secretly entered or joined (a group or organization) to get information or do harm. (घुसपैठ करना)

questions.question: Which word refers to graceful and balanced behavior?
questions.explanation: B) Poise (noun) – Graceful, balanced and confident behavior संतुलित, सुसंयमित और गरिमापूर्ण व्यवहार • Clumsy (adj.) – Awkward or lacking grace अनाड़ी / भद्दा • Grit (noun) – Courage and determination साहस और दृढ़ता • Frenzy (noun) – Wild or uncontrolled excitement उन्माद / उतेजना

questions.question: Choose the misspelt word from the given options.
questions.explanation: C) 'Ignoreamus' गलत वर्तनी है — इसकी सही वर्तनी 'Ignoramus' है जिसका अर्थ है 'मूर्ख या अज्ञानी व्यक्ति' (an ignorant or stupid person). जबकि 'Independence' (स्वतंत्रता), 'Connotation' (निहितार्थ), और Retribution (प्रतिकार) — ये तीनों सही वर्तनी में हैं। 'Ignoreamus' is misspelled — the correct spelling is 'Ignoramus' meaning "an ignorant or stupid person." 'Independence' (the state of being free from control), 'Connotation' (an idea or feeling a word invokes beyond its literal meaning), and 'Retribution (punishment for a crime) are all correctly spelled. ✅ Correct answer: Ignoreamus

questions.question: What term refers to a strong feeling of not liking someone or something?
questions.explanation: B) Hatred (noun) – A strong feeling of intense dislike for someone or something घृणा/नफ़रत • Affection (noun) – A feeling of fondness or liking स्नेह • Admiration (noun) – Respect or approval for someone or something प्रशंसा • Love (noun) – A deep feeling of care or attachment प्रेम

questions.question: Which of the following options is the most suitable conversion of the following sentence into Active Voice? Will the project have been completed by the research team before the deadline?
options: Has the research team completed the project before the deadline?
Is the research team completing the project before the deadline?
Will the research team complete the project before the deadline?
Will the research team have completed the project before the deadline?
questions.explanation: D) Will the research team have completed the project before the deadline? Passive से Active Voice में बदलने के लिए, Passive वाक्य का Subject Active वाक्य का Object बन जाता है और "by + doer" Active वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है। Verb को Passive से Active में बदलते समय सही Tense का ध्यान रखा जाता है। यहाँ "the project" Subject है, "will have been completed" (Future Perfect Passive) Verb है और "by the research team" doer है। Active में बदलने पर "the research team" Subject बन जाता है, Verb "will have completed" (Future Perfect) हो जाता है और "the project" Object बन जाता है। साथ ही, प्रश्नवाचक संरचना बनाए रखने के लिए "Will" से वाक्य शुरू होता है। इसलिए सही वाक्य है — "Will the research team have completed the project before the deadline?" To change a sentence from Passive to Active Voice, the subject of the passive sentence becomes the object, and the doer (introduced by "by") becomes the subject. The verb is converted into the correct active tense. In this case, "the project" (Subject), "will have been completed" (Future Perfect Passive), and "by the research team" are transformed into "the research team" (Subject), "will have completed" (Active Verb in Future Perfect), and "the project" (Object). The interrogative structure is maintained by starting with "Will". Hence, the correct answer is — "Will the research team have completed the project before the deadline?" (Option 4)

questions.question: Which of the following options is the most suitable conversion of the following sentence into Active Voice? Why aren’t benign tumours rejected by the body?
options: Why don't the body reject benign tumours?
Why won't the body reject benign tumours?
Why doesn't the body reject benign tumours?
Why can't the body reject benign tumours?
questions.explanation: C) Why doesn't the body reject benign tumours? Passive से Active Voice में बदलने के लिए, Passive वाक्य का Subject Active वाक्य का Object बन जाता है और "by + doer" Active वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है। Verb को Passive से Active में बदलते समय सही Tense और Auxiliary Verb का ध्यान रखा जाता है। यहाँ "benign tumours" Subject है, "aren’t rejected" (Present Simple Passive) Verb है और "by the body" doer है। Active में बदलने पर "the body" Subject बन जाता है, Verb "doesn’t reject" (Present Simple Negative) हो जाता है और "benign tumours" Object बन जाता है। साथ ही, प्रश्नवाचक संरचना बनाए रखने के लिए "Why doesn’t" से वाक्य शुरू होता है। इसलिए सही वाक्य है — "Why doesn’t the body reject benign tumours?" To change a sentence from Passive to Active Voice, the subject of the passive sentence becomes the object, and the doer (introduced by "by") becomes the subject. The verb is converted into the correct active form with proper auxiliary usage. In this case, "benign tumours" (Subject), "aren’t rejected" (Present Simple Passive), and "by the body" are transformed into "the body" (Subject), "doesn’t reject" (Active Verb in Present Simple Negative), and "benign tumours" (Object). The interrogative form is maintained by starting with "Why doesn’t". Hence, the correct answer is — "Why doesn’t the body reject benign tumours?" (Option 3)

questions.question: Choose the correct indirect narration of the following statement from the given alternatives: The officer said, "The library will be supplied with sufficient number of books within one month."
options: The officer said that the library would be supplied with sufficient number of books within one month.
The officer said that the library must be supplied with sufficient number of books within one month.
The officer said that the library has to be supplied with sufficient number of books within one month.
The officer said that the library ought to be supplied with sufficient number of books within one month.
questions.explanation: A) . The officer said that the library would be supplied with sufficient number of books within one month. Direct से Indirect Narration में बदलते समय Reporting Verb, Tense और Modal Verb में परिवर्तन किया जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में Reporting Verb "said" Past Tense में है, इसलिए Reported Speech का Future Tense (will) बदलकर Future in the Past (would) हो जाता है। Direct वाक्य में "will be supplied" (Future Passive) प्रयोग हुआ है, जो Indirect में बदलकर "would be supplied" हो जाता है। बाकी वाक्य संरचना वही रहती है। To convert Direct Speech into Indirect Speech, the tense of the reported clause changes according to the reporting verb. Since the reporting verb "said" is in the past tense, the modal "will" changes to "would". The passive construction is retained, so "will be supplied" correctly becomes "would be supplied" in Indirect Narration.

questions.question: Select the correct alternative to fill in the blank with the correct preposition/prepositional phrase in the following sentence. Prepare the project ________ the instructions given in the manual.
questions.explanation: B) According to’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि sentence में “instructions given in the manual” के अनुसार काम करने की बात कही जा रही है। यहाँ “prepare the project ________ the instructions” का अर्थ है कि प्रोजेक्ट को दिए गए निर्देशों के अनुसार तैयार करना। इसलिए, “according to” सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प होगा। • ‘According to’ should be used because the sentence conveys the idea of following the instructions given in the manual. The phrase “prepare the project ________ the instructions” means to make the project in conformity with the given guidelines. Thus, “according to” would be the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Select the correct alternative to fill in the blank with the correct preposition in the following sentence. The secretary has signed the document ________ the chairman of the selection committee.
questions.explanation: C) for’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि sentence में “signed the document ______ the chairman” का अर्थ है कि secretary ने chairman की ओर से (on behalf of) document पर हस्ताक्षर किए हैं। यहाँ ‘for’ किसी के behalf या representation को दर्शाता है, जो इस संदर्भ में सही बैठता है। इसलिए, ‘for’ सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प होगा। • ‘for’ should be used because in the sentence “signed the document ______ the chairman”, it implies that the secretary signed the document on behalf of the chairman. The preposition ‘for’ is used to indicate representation or acting in place of someone. Thus, ‘for’ would be the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of homonyms can be used to fill in the blanks most suitably? He had to ____ his brains trying to remember how he came to be on the shore strewn with ____ and driftwood.
questions.explanation: D) ‘rack, wrack’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि पूरे संदर्भ में sentence में पहले भाग में “rack his brains” एक fixed idiomatic expression है जिसका अर्थ है बहुत ज़ोर डालकर सोचना। दूसरे भाग में “shore strewn with wrack” में “wrack” का अर्थ समुद्र किनारे पड़ी समुद्री घास, अवशेष या drift material होता है। इसलिए पहला blank “rack” और दूसरा “wrack” सबसे उपयुक्त हैं। • **‘rack, wrack’ should be used because in the entire context, the first part uses the fixed idiom “rack his brains”, meaning to think intensely or strain one’s mind. In the second part, “shore strewn with wrack” uses “wrack” meaning seaweed or debris washed ashore. Thus, “rack” fits the first blank and “wrack” fits the second most appropriately.

questions.question: Choose the best option to replace the underlined part to improve the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select 'No substitution.' The villagers are (suffering with) a shortage of clean water.
questions.explanation: C) ‘suffering with’ के बदले ‘suffering from’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि ‘suffer’ के साथ सही Preposition ‘from’ आती है, जो किसी समस्या, बीमारी या कमी को दर्शाती है; जैसे— People are suffering from poverty. • ‘suffering from’ will be used instead of ‘suffering with’ because the correct preposition used with ‘suffer’ to show pain, problem, or lack is ‘from’; Like— People are suffering from poverty.

questions.question: Rearrange the following sentences in correct order to make a meaningful sentence P. were circulated among industry stakeholders Q. to gather structured input before finalization R. the draft policy guidelines S. prepared by the inter-ministerial panel
questions.explanation: D) RSQP. R starts the sentence by introducing the subject: “The draft policy guidelines.” This tells us what the sentence is about — the main entity that performs or receives the action. R sentence की शुरुआत subject “The draft policy guidelines” से करता है, जो बताता है कि sentence किसके बारे में है — वह main entity जो action perform या receive करती है। S follows “R” as it adds a past participle phrase “prepared by the inter-ministerial panel,” which describes who created the draft guidelines. This gives essential background information about the subject. This establishes a clear noun-plus-modifier relationship: The draft policy guidelines prepared by the inter-ministerial panel. S, R के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह past participle phrase “prepared by the inter-ministerial panel” जोड़ता है, जो बताता है कि draft guidelines किसने बनाईं। यह subject के बारे में essential background information देता है। इससे स्पष्ट noun-plus-modifier relationship बनता है: The draft policy guidelines prepared by the inter-ministerial panel. Q follows “S” as it introduces the infinitive phrase “to gather structured input before finalization,” which explains the purpose of circulating the guidelines. This tells us why the action was done. Q, S के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह infinitive phrase “to gather structured input before finalization” introduce करता है, जो guidelines के circulate करने का purpose बताता है। यह बताता है कि action क्यों किया गया। P follows “Q” as it introduces the main verb phrase “were circulated among industry stakeholders,” which completes the sentence by telling us what happened to the draft guidelines. This is the core action of the sentence. P, Q के बाद naturally आता है क्योंकि यह main verb phrase “were circulated among industry stakeholders” introduce करता है, जो sentence को complete करता है और बताता है कि draft guidelines के साथ क्या हुआ। यह sentence का core action है।

questions.question: Read the passage below and choose the correct word for each blank. A responsible citizen is not defined merely by legal obligations but also by their contribution to the community. Civic sense includes following rules, respecting public spaces, and participating in social causes. When citizens (1) ______ basic responsibilities, society begins to suffer. Littering, ignoring traffic rules, or staying silent in the face of injustice are signs of weak civic values. It is important to understand that progress depends on (2) ______ efforts. Educated individuals must not remain passive observers but take the lead in creating awareness. Acts like voting, volunteering, and speaking against wrongdoing can lead to meaningful change. Social responsibility is not just a duty—it is a (3) ______. When people unite with a shared vision, communities grow stronger. The real strength of democracy lies in an informed and (4) ______ citizenry that upholds justice, equality, and the (5) ______ good. What will come in (1)?
questions.explanation: B) overlook ‘Overlook’ का use होगा क्योंकि sentence में कहा गया है कि जब नागरिक basic responsibilities को नज़रअंदाज़ करते हैं, तो society को नुकसान होता है। Overlook का अर्थ होता है किसी ज़रूरी बात को अनदेखा करना या ध्यान न देना। Context में littering, traffic rules को ignore करना और injustice पर चुप रहना जैसे उदाहरण दिए गए हैं, जो सीधे तौर पर responsibilities को ignore करने को दर्शाते हैं। इसलिए overlook यहाँ अर्थ और grammar दोनों के अनुसार सही है। जबकि abandon का अर्थ पूरी तरह त्याग देना, demand का अर्थ माँग करना और delay का अर्थ टालना होता है, जो इस sentence के भाव से मेल नहीं खाते। • ‘Overlook’ will be used because it means to ignore or fail to notice something important. The sentence explains that when citizens ignore their basic responsibilities, society suffers. Hence, overlook fits the contextual meaning and grammatical structure, whereas abandon, demand, and delay do not align with the idea of neglecting civic duties.

questions.question: Read the passage below and choose the correct word for each blank. A responsible citizen is not defined merely by legal obligations but also by their contribution to the community. Civic sense includes following rules, respecting public spaces, and participating in social causes. When citizens (1) ______ basic responsibilities, society begins to suffer. Littering, ignoring traffic rules, or staying silent in the face of injustice are signs of weak civic values. It is important to understand that progress depends on (2) ______ efforts. Educated individuals must not remain passive observers but take the lead in creating awareness. Acts like voting, volunteering, and speaking against wrongdoing can lead to meaningful change. Social responsibility is not just a duty—it is a (3) ______. When people unite with a shared vision, communities grow stronger. The real strength of democracy lies in an informed and (4) ______ citizenry that upholds justice, equality, and the (5) ______ good. What will come in (2)?
questions.explanation: C) collective ‘Collective’ का use होगा क्योंकि sentence में कहा गया है कि progress depends on (2) ______ efforts। यहाँ प्रगति को किसी एक व्यक्ति के प्रयास से नहीं, बल्कि समाज के लोगों के मिलकर किए गए प्रयासों से जोड़ा गया है। इसलिए collective — यानी सामूहिक — इस context में सबसे उपयुक्त है। जबकि physical का अर्थ शारीरिक, solo का अर्थ अकेले और creative का अर्थ रचनात्मक होता है, जो प्रगति को समाजिक सहयोग से जोड़ने वाले अर्थ को सही तरह से व्यक्त नहीं करते। ‘Collective’ will be used because it refers to shared or joint efforts made by people together. The sentence emphasizes that societal progress depends on combined actions rather than individual, physical, or merely creative efforts. Hence, collective fits both grammatically and contextually.

questions.question: Read the passage below and choose the correct word for each blank. A responsible citizen is not defined merely by legal obligations but also by their contribution to the community. Civic sense includes following rules, respecting public spaces, and participating in social causes. When citizens (1) ______ basic responsibilities, society begins to suffer. Littering, ignoring traffic rules, or staying silent in the face of injustice are signs of weak civic values. It is important to understand that progress depends on (2) ______ efforts. Educated individuals must not remain passive observers but take the lead in creating awareness. Acts like voting, volunteering, and speaking against wrongdoing can lead to meaningful change. Social responsibility is not just a duty—it is a (3) ______. When people unite with a shared vision, communities grow stronger. The real strength of democracy lies in an informed and (4) ______ citizenry that upholds justice, equality, and the (5) ______ good. What will come in (3)?
questions.explanation: B) privilege ‘Privilege’ का use होगा क्योंकि sentence में कहा गया है कि Social responsibility is not just a duty—it is a (3) ______. यहाँ यह बताया जा रहा है कि सामाजिक ज़िम्मेदारी को बोझ की तरह नहीं, बल्कि एक सकारात्मक और सम्मानजनक अवसर की तरह देखा जाना चाहिए। Privilege का अर्थ होता है ऐसा अवसर या सम्मान जो किसी को प्राप्त हो। इसलिए यह word context और grammar दोनों के अनुसार सही है। जबकि burden का अर्थ बोझ, reaction का अर्थ प्रतिक्रिया और complaint का अर्थ शिकायत होता है, जो इस सकारात्मक भाव से मेल नहीं खाते। ‘Privilege’ will be used because it conveys the idea that social responsibility is something valuable and honorable, not something imposed or negative. The sentence contrasts duty with privilege, emphasizing a positive civic outlook. Hence, privilege fits grammatically and contextually, whereas burden, reaction, and complaint do not.

questions.question: Read the passage below and choose the correct word for each blank. A responsible citizen is not defined merely by legal obligations but also by their contribution to the community. Civic sense includes following rules, respecting public spaces, and participating in social causes. When citizens (1) ______ basic responsibilities, society begins to suffer. Littering, ignoring traffic rules, or staying silent in the face of injustice are signs of weak civic values. It is important to understand that progress depends on (2) ______ efforts. Educated individuals must not remain passive observers but take the lead in creating awareness. Acts like voting, volunteering, and speaking against wrongdoing can lead to meaningful change. Social responsibility is not just a duty—it is a (3) ______. When people unite with a shared vision, communities grow stronger. The real strength of democracy lies in an informed and (4) ______ citizenry that upholds justice, equality, and the (5) ______ good. What will come in (4)?
questions.explanation: C) responsible ‘Responsible’ का use होगा क्योंकि sentence में कहा गया है कि लोकतंत्र की वास्तविक शक्ति an informed and (4) ______ citizenry में निहित है, जो justice, equality और common good को uphold करती है। Responsible नागरिक वह होता है जो अपने कर्तव्यों को समझता है और समाज के प्रति जवाबदेह रहता है, इसलिए यह शब्द अर्थ और grammar दोनों के अनुसार सही है। जबकि passive का अर्थ निष्क्रिय, indifferent का अर्थ उदासीन और careless का अर्थ लापरवाह होता है, जो sentence के सकारात्मक भाव से मेल नहीं खाते। ‘Responsible’ will be used because the sentence states that the real strength of democracy lies in an informed and (4) ______ citizenry that upholds justice, equality, and the common good. A responsible citizen is aware of civic duties and actively contributes to society, which fits the positive and normative tone of the sentence. In contrast, passive, indifferent, and careless all convey neglect or lack of concern, which directly contradicts the idea of upholding democratic values.

questions.question: Read the passage below and choose the correct word for each blank. A responsible citizen is not defined merely by legal obligations but also by their contribution to the community. Civic sense includes following rules, respecting public spaces, and participating in social causes. When citizens (1) ______ basic responsibilities, society begins to suffer. Littering, ignoring traffic rules, or staying silent in the face of injustice are signs of weak civic values. It is important to understand that progress depends on (2) ______ efforts. Educated individuals must not remain passive observers but take the lead in creating awareness. Acts like voting, volunteering, and speaking against wrongdoing can lead to meaningful change. Social responsibility is not just a duty—it is a (3) ______. When people unite with a shared vision, communities grow stronger. The real strength of democracy lies in an informed and (4) ______ citizenry that upholds justice, equality, and the (5) ______ good. What will come in (5)?
questions.explanation: D) common ‘Common’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि सही और प्रचलित वाक्यांश “common good” होता है, जिसका अर्थ है समाज के सभी लोगों का सामूहिक कल्याण। वाक्य में justice, equality और नागरिकों की सामूहिक जिम्मेदारी की बात की गई है, इसलिए common शब्द अर्थ और grammar दोनों के अनुसार बिल्कुल सही है। जबकि total, private, और higher शब्द good के साथ इस civic context में सही अर्थ नहीं देते 'Common’ will be used because the fixed expression is “common good”, which refers to the welfare or well-being of all members of a society. The sentence talks about justice, equality, and the shared benefit of society, so common fits both idiomatically and contextually. In contrast, total, private, and higher do not form a meaningful or accepted collocation with good in this civic context.
